name: The Frozen Heart
author: Martin Turner
summary: A classic story-driven total conversion — start a new pilot and head to the bar on Thallos.
description: A story-heavy total conversion originally created for the first Escape Velocity and later ported to the Nova engine by Andrew Simmonds. Start a new pilot, land on Thallos, and visit the bar to begin. Includes its own documents folder with two encrypted files you're given access to later in the story — read it before you start.

name: Femme Fatale
author: Martin Turner
summary: The sequel to The Frozen Heart — a new pilot lands on Yëni Istanbul to begin.
description: The direct sequel to The Frozen Heart, also by Martin Turner and ported to the Nova engine by Andrew Simmonds. Start a new pilot, land on Yëni Istanbul, and visit the bar. Read the included documentation first.

name: Polycon EV
author: AnubisTTP
summary: A large original total conversion with its own galaxy, ships, and storyline.
description: A full total conversion built from scratch by AnubisTTP with an original setting, galaxy, ships, and missions, distinct from the stock EV Nova universe. Quick-play launcher included, or install manually by swapping in the supplied Nova Files.

name: EV Classic for Nova
author: Ambrosia Software (original); ported by andrews05
summary: The original Escape Velocity, faithfully ported onto the Nova engine.
description: A port of Ambrosia Software's original Escape Velocity — the first game in the series — onto the EV Nova engine, designed to look and feel as close to the 1996 original as possible. Includes optional fix plug-ins (manual flares, an AI rocket-range hack, and a bundle of gameplay bug fixes) alongside the main scenario. Requires the included "Sillycon" font for authentic interface text.

name: EV Override for Nova
author: Ambrosia Software (original); ported by andrews05
summary: The second game in the series, EV Override, ported onto the Nova engine.
description: A port of Ambrosia Software's EV Override — the second game in the series — onto the EV Nova engine. Designed to match the original's look and feel; several other catalog entries (EVO Extras, EVO Facelift, Cold Fusion for Nova) build on top of this one. Requires the included "Sillycon" font.

name: rEV
author: Kris Quistorff; rebuilt by andrews05
summary: A major graphics and feature-enhancement pack for EV Classic for Nova.
description: A graphical facelift and feature-enhancement pack for EV Classic for Nova, originally created by Kris Quistorff and rebuilt/expanded by andrews05. Ships as several optional pieces: rEV Data (small balanced feature enhancements, recommended), rEV xData (new outfits/weapons/ship variants), and rEV XL Ships (larger, more accurately-scaled ship sprites, best paired with xData). Install after EV Classic for Nova.

name: Cold Fusion for Nova
author: Dreamwave Studios; converted by Dr. Trowel
summary: A complete graphics replacement for EV Override, in thousands of colors.
description: A full graphics replacement for EV Override — re-textured ships, outfits, planets/stations, explosions, asteroids, and interface art — rendered in thousands of colors rather than the original's dithered 256. No new ships, outfits, or missions, just a visual overhaul. Ships as six separate pieces (interface, planets, ships in space, shipyard/outfitter pics, shots & rocks, sparse planets) so you can pick which parts you want; install on top of EV Override for Nova.

name: EVO Facelift
author: Tim Morgan (Lightning Bolt Software); repackaged by andrews05
summary: A graphical facelift for EV Override — map colors, new station art, a splash screen, and more.
description: A graphical facelift for EV Override for Nova, originally made by Tim Morgan of Lightning Bolt Software and repackaged for distribution by andrews05, with some fixes and rescaled sprites. Adds map colors, reorganized shipyard/outfitter art, new station sprites, and a 16-bit splash screen; bundles in Cold Fusion Planets and Sounds Galore. Not every component is required — Sounds Galore and the Range Extension plug are optional extras.

name: EVO Extras
author: andrews05
summary: Six small gameplay plug-ins for EV Override, from bug-fix-style tweaks to a new UE mission string.
description: A collection of six small, independent plug-ins for EV Override for Nova: Alien Artifact (reworks a classic Igadzra mission), Bonus Bays (unlocks purchasing UE/Voinian/Zidagar fighter bays), Mischievous Mods (a grab-bag of small annoyance fixes), Paaren Proceedings (a roughly 10-mission UE side story, with a quick-start pilot background to jump straight in), and Super Special (lets you keep extra weapons/shielding captured from certain named AI ships). Each comes in Facelift and non-Facelift variants; install only the ones you want.

name: Shields
author: andrews05
summary: A small plug-in adjusting ship shields, with a dedicated variant for ARPIA2.
description: A small gameplay plug-in that adjusts ship shield behavior. Ships with two variants: a standard version and one specifically built for compatibility with the ARPIA2 total conversion — install whichever matches the scenario you're playing.

name: Collision Damage
author: andrews05
summary: Ships take damage when they physically collide with each other.
description: A small gameplay plug-in that adds damage when ships collide with one another in flight — stock EV Nova has no collision damage between ships, so this adds a bit of extra risk to crowded dogfights and close flying.

name: Dodge That
author: andrews05
summary: Loosens missile tracking so incoming rockets are easier to dodge.
description: A small gameplay plug-in that reduces missile/rocket tracking accuracy, giving the player more of a chance to physically dodge incoming ordnance rather than relying purely on point-defense or countermeasures.

name: ARPIA2
author: Peter "Pace" Craddock and the ARPIA Team
summary: An acclaimed, puzzle-driven total conversion with a new galaxy, governments, and branching story.
description: A large, highly-regarded total conversion by Peter "Pace" Craddock and the ARPIA Team (graphics, ship art, planet art, mission design credited across the team in-readme). Features an original galaxy, new governments, and a branching story with missions that expect the player to actually think — read the included ReadMe before diving in, especially for the riddle-solving sections. Do not delete any of the extra files/folders the plug-in installs alongside itself.

name: White Dwarf
author: DrFive
summary: A fan sequel set a century after Nova, in a galaxy still recovering from a mysterious Calamity.
description: A fan sequel total conversion by DrFive, set roughly a hundred years after the events of Nova (and after the same author's Brave New Void). The galaxy is still recovering from a mysterious "Calamity" that warped and ravaged known space seventy years earlier — and something may be stirring beyond the hyperlanes. In open beta as of this writing; expect an actively-developed, still-evolving scenario.

name: Brave New Void
author: DrFive
summary: A large-scale rebalance and expansion of the base Nova scenario, not a new galaxy.
description: A "total expansion" plug-in for the base EV Nova scenario by DrFive (the same author as White Dwarf): rebalances combat, fixes bugs and design issues in the original game, and adds new ships, missions, outfits, and places to explore that aim to feel native to the Nova universe. Many of the stronger ship variants are gated behind side missions, hidden upgrades, or rare shipyards rather than being available outright.

name: Starfleet Adventures
author: Uncle Twitchy, later Dr. Trowel and astor
summary: A Star Trek: The Original Series total conversion with an unusually large map and no fighters.
description: A total conversion recasting Nova as 23rd-century Star Trek (The Original Series and the early movies). Notable design twists: no fighters, mostly Federation ships, "upgrades" come from hiring command crew rather than buying hardware, and most ships are inertialess, which changes combat significantly from stock Nova. Started by Uncle Twitchy and later carried on by Dr. Trowel and then astor; development has been quiet since its public alpha, so treat this as an unfinished but substantial labor of love rather than a polished release.

name: Ashen Galaxy
author: gray_shirt_ninja (graphics by Meowx)
summary: A combat-focused human-vs-alien total conversion with dozens of ships across 200+ systems.
description: A total conversion built around a thin but serviceable plot — humans fighting a technologically advanced alien threat — with gameplay centered heavily on combat. Adds dozens of original ships and weapons using Meowx's graphics set, spread across more than two hundred systems, with a campaign to keep you oriented while you fight.

name: Brotherhood of the Kestrel
author: EV Nova community (consolidates the classic "Empire" plug-in series)
summary: A consolidation of the classic "Empire" series of plug-ins into one total conversion.
description: Brings together the long-running "Empire" series of community plug-ins (originally for EV Classic) into a single consolidated total conversion for the Nova engine. Attribution for the underlying Empire-series work is spread across multiple original contributors from the classic-EV era; this package is the community's effort to preserve and unify it.

name: Acheron
author: Crusader Alpha
summary: A small, focused total conversion set in the Trikeratos Alliance, favoring depth over breadth.
description: A compact total conversion by Crusader Alpha, released in 2008, that completely replaces the Nova scenario with new ships, weapons, missions, outfits, and graphics inside a deliberately small galaxy (the Trikeratos Alliance). Aims for depth over breadth — a tighter, more focused scenario rather than a sprawling one.

name: Realm of Prey
author: Tim Isles; ported to Nova by Agee (MacDevil)
summary: One of the best-regarded expansions for the original Escape Velocity, ported to the Nova engine.
description: Originally one of the standout expansion sets for the first Escape Velocity, created by Tim Isles, later ported onto the Nova engine by Agee (MacDevil). Play it as its own scenario, distinct from stock Nova.

name: Pale
author: EV Nova community
summary: An ambitious expansion to EV Classic.
description: Billed by the community as "an ambitious expansion to EV Classic" — install on top of EV Classic for Nova. Specific authorship details weren't confirmed while building this catalog entry; treat it as a community-maintained expansion rather than an officially attributed one.

name: Reign of the UE
author: UE Patriot
summary: Continues the UE storyline after one of EV Override's major objectives is completed.
description: An expansion plug-in by UE Patriot, originally written for the classic EV Override and since adapted for EV Override for Nova, that continues the UE storyline past one of Override's major campaign milestones.

name: EV Game Expander
author: EV Nova community
summary: An all-new scenario that substantially expands EV Classic.
description: Expands EV Classic for Nova with a substantially larger game environment. Specific authorship details weren't confirmed while building this catalog entry; treat it as a community-maintained expansion rather than an officially attributed one.
